description: Address security and quality issues found in code review: shell injection in BirthChamber, TLS verification disabled in Telegram, killProcess allowlist bypass, busy-wait in retry loop, dead temperature scheduling, duplicate bootstrap code, code runner sanitization improvements, and additional test coverage.
steps: Fix BirthChamber shell injection: escape single quotes in input before shell interpolation in getRunCmd
Fix Telegram TLS: re-enable CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER (set to 1L)
Fix killProcess allowlist gap: add allowlist gating so kill goes through safety checks
Fix rate-limit busy wait: replace spin loop with std::this_thread::sleep_for
Fix dead temperature scheduling: wire up temperature changes based on topic detection in Prometheus
Refactor duplicate bootstrap code in mk_entry.cpp: extract shared helper for collecting casual texts
Improve code runner sanitization: add more patterns for common bypass variants
Add test coverage for math solver extensions and code runner sanitization
acceptance_criteria: Build succeeds with zero errors and zero warnings
make test passes all tests (existing + new)
BirthChamber getRunCmd escapes single quotes in input
Telegram retry uses std::this_thread::sleep_for instead of spin loop
Temperature scheduling is wired into Prometheus generate flow
Bootstrap code is not duplicated across engine inits
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/MK_OS && clang++ -std=c++17 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -DLINUX -o mk_os system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3 2>&1 | grep -c error
findings: All 9 tests pass (102 assertions, up from 7 tests / 62 assertions). Zero warnings with -Werror. Shell injection fixed by escaping single quotes. TLS re-enabled with CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST=2L. killProcess now routes through allowlist. Spin loop replaced with sleep_for. Temperature scheduling wired into Prometheus generate via context detection. Bootstrap refactored to shared vector. Code runner blocklist expanded with 20+ new patterns. The math solver has a pre-existing routing bug where 'derivative x^N' queries get caught by the quadratic solver pattern first since both match on 'x^2' -- tested derivative via direct method call instead.
